name: Charm
shortDescription: Charm, also known as Eros (Roman Cupid), is the Greek god of love, desire, and attraction. He is often depicted as Aphrodite's son and plays a significant role in the affairs of gods and mortals alike.
description: Charm, more commonly known as Eros, is the Greek god of love, desire, attraction, and affection. Often depicted as the son of Aphrodite, the goddess of love and beauty, his role varies across different accounts. In early accounts, Eros is a primordial deity, a fundamental force of creation, born from Chaos along with Gaia (Earth) and Tartarus (the Underworld). In later mythology, he becomes a mischievous and often capricious god, wielding arrows that inspire irresistible love or aversion in those he strikes. He is a powerful figure, capable of influencing both gods and mortals. The myth of Eros and Psyche is one of the most famous stories associated with him, depicting a complex and transformative love that overcomes obstacles. The story highlights the power of love, trust, and perseverance. Eros's influence permeates many aspects of Greek mythology and art, often representing the potent and sometimes uncontrollable nature of desire. He is associated with both physical and emotional love, embodying the irresistible force that binds individuals together. He is an important figure in understanding the Greek perception of love and its influence on human behavior and the divine realm.
